extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is true only when C = (seizure_history AND qt_prolongation AND renal_impairment AND hypertension) holds, since that conjunction rule (priority 50) is the sole rule setting it true and the default is false. rec_drug_b is governed by two rules: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, fires on seizure_history -> true) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, fires on C -> false). Whenever rec_drug_a is true, C holds, so guard_suppress_b (priority 90) fires and beats rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) on per-output priority resolution, forcing rec_drug_b := false. Thus rec_drug_a true always implies rec_drug_b false. Equivalently, rec_drug_b true requires seizure_history true while C is false (else suppressed), and when C is false rec_drug_a defaults to false. The guard condition is identical to the rec_drug_a condition and its strictly higher priority (90 > 50) makes the conjunction mutually exclusive across the entire input space. The property (never rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true) holds for all 64 input combinations.
